entries.title: BORED APE YACHT CLUB | Website Redesign

messages.title: Technical Program Manager, Knowledge Systems
messages.message:
The AI-assisted work problem you're describing (output goes everywhere, nothing connects) is the exact thing I spent a project solving. I built a source-of-truth system and orchestration agent to keep AI work connected instead of scattered across docs: https://lab26.randallfransen.com/phred. Structure first, then accelerate.

I also ran production for Base at Coinbase, so the surge cadence and remote-first rhythm aren't new to me. I've done the CMS, metadata, and cross-functional wrangling side too, most recently as Director of Product at an agentic AI startup.

I'm good at jumping in mid-stride and keeping distributed ownership from turning into chaos. Happy to talk through the Knowledge Systems roadmap.

insertedExample.oneHitter: Project Hub for Retrieval, Execution, & Delivery

messages.title: Staff Technical Program Manager, CX Agent Experience
messages.message: I was Production Lead for Base at Coinbase, so "remote-first with quarterly surges" and the pace of that environment aren't a surprise to me. Agent Cockpit sits right in my wheelhouse: internal platform, technical dependencies moving under you, compliance users who need the thing to just work every day. I spend most of my time jumping in mid-stride, sorting the intake and prioritization, and keeping cross-functional teams (Eng, Product, Security) pointed the same direction without stalling. On the AI side, I built P.H.R.E.D., an operating system that keeps AI-assisted work connected and humans in control of scope and privacy instead of scattered across a dozen docs: https://lab26.randallfransen.com/phred. Happy to talk through where Cockpit is bottlenecking.

insertedExample.oneHitter: Project Hub for Retrieval, Execution, & Delivery

messages.title: Brand Producer
messages.location: San Francisco, CA • New York, NY • United States
messages.message: Onboarding agencies and freelancers, building timelines that let creatives just create, keeping the whole thing from going sideways: that's the seat I've lived in for 15+ years. At AKQA I ran as Program Director on Nike and Trek, and at Coinbase I was Production Lead for Base. Fast, fluid, ambiguous, small teams stretched thin, that's my natural habitat. I jump in mid-stride and navigate the choppy stuff before it capsizes the work. Would love to talk about the Brand Producer role and what shipping high-impact brand initiatives looks like on the Brand Studio team.

messages.title: Senior Programs & Business Operations Lead, Airbnb Services
messages.message:
I've done a tour through Airbnb before as a contract Executive Producer, so "co-develop and execute" from concept to launch is familiar ground. The connective-leader-without-authority part is where I actually live: as Program Director at AKQA I ran cross-functional planning across Nike and Trek, and I steered the Base launch at Coinbase, where a new category and aggressive timelines were the whole weather system.

Building the operating structure before you accelerate the work is the thing I care most about. That's the methodology behind P.H.R.E.D., where the goal was keeping small teams connected instead of scattered: https://lab26.randallfransen.com/phred

Worth a conversation about the emerging categories work?

insertedExample.oneHitter: Project Hub for Retrieval, Execution, & Delivery

messages.title: Product Operations Manager
messages.message:
The part of this that jumps out to me: coordinating launches end-to-end while building agentic workflows so the output doesn't scatter. I just built exactly that. Small team, confidential product, aggressive timeline, and an orchestration agent that kept AI-assisted work connected instead of piled up across a dozen half-dead docs. Wrote up the methodology here: https://lab26.randallfransen.com/phred

Before that I spent 15+ years as an EP running multidisciplinary teams, including the Base launch at Coinbase, so being the liaison between product, GTM, and everyone with an opinion is familiar ground. Happy to talk through how it'd map to Notion.

insertedExample.oneHitter: Project Hub for Retrieval, Execution, & Delivery

messages.title: Creative Director, Growth
messages.message:
Growth creative that has to move fast without turning into slop is a specific kind of hard, and it's most of what I've been doing lately. I spent 15+ years running multidisciplinary teams and directing agencies (Program Director at AKQA on Nike and Trek, Production Lead for Base at Coinbase), so setting direction and getting the best out of external partners is home turf.

The newer piece: I built a whole operating methodology for AI-assisted work, Project OS, because the output goes everywhere and nothing connects unless you build structure first. Here's the writeup: https://lab26.randallfransen.com/phred

Growth work at OpenAI's speed is exactly where that thinking earns its keep. Worth a chat?

insertedExample.oneHitter: Project Hub for Retrieval, Execution, & Delivery

messages.title: Marketing Events Producer
messages.location: San Francisco, CA | New York City, NY
messages.message:
You're producing live moments for a company whose whole product is AI, and I'd bet most of your audience still thinks AI is magic. I've spent 15+ years running digital production for teams that had to explain complicated tech to skeptical rooms: Coinbase, Mozilla, Nike, Trek. On the Mozilla side we built a Gen Z-coded activation that connected digital and IRL over a three-month run (https://www.randallfransen.com/work/billionaire-blast-off-mozilla), which is roughly the run-of-show-and-vendors-and-stage juggle your posting describes.

I jump in mid-stride, build the process, wrangle the vendors, and keep the show calm when it wants to go sideways. Happy to talk.

insertedExample.oneHitter: Create a Gen Z-coded moment to amplify Open What You Want.

messages.title: Strategy & Operations Manager, Consumer Revenue
messages.message:
I've spent the last stretch building process where none existed, which is most of what this role sounds like. At Coinbase I was Production Lead for Base, standing up structure and moving deals and launches through Product, Legal, and Finance without ever holding direct authority over any of them. Before that I managed a $14MM retainer for HTC, which taught me how partnership economics actually behave when a deal hits a spreadsheet.

The deal desk part is the fun bit for me: defining red lines, approval frameworks, keeping ambiguous asks from turning into chaos. Web3 and gaming-adjacent worlds are home turf too. Happy to talk about how I'd stand up Consumer Revenue ops from scratch.

messages.title: Professional Services Program Manager
messages.message:
You need someone who can walk into a tangled, multi-workstream engagement mid-stride and get it moving toward outcomes people actually agreed on. That's the work I keep getting hired to do. As Program Director at AKQA I ran interconnected client programs for Nike and Trek, and at Coinbase I was Production Lead for Base, coordinating delivery across technical and creative teams in an all-remote setup.

The DevOps side is newer to me than accounts and film, but keeping scattered work connected is the whole reason I built P.H.R.E.D., an operating system for AI-assisted delivery: https://lab26.randallfransen.com/phred. Structure first, then speed.

Happy to talk if the fit feels right.

insertedExample.oneHitter: Project Hub for Retrieval, Execution, & Delivery

messages.title: Viral Creative Producer
messages.message: The Brian glass-box run is the kind of thing I chase: earn attention, don't buy it. I've spent 15+ years running stunts and activations end-to-end, most recently for Mozilla, where we built a Gen Z-coded Q4 moment that punched up at Big Tech and connected digital to IRL over a three-month run (https://www.randallfransen.com/work/billionaire-blast-off-mozilla). Concept, talent, permits, vendors, capture, distribution, the whole messy pipeline. I've also managed real budgets: a $14MM social retainer, so the money side doesn't scare me. If your backlog of bigger swings needs a producer who lands them, I'm around.

insertedExample.oneHitter: Create a Gen Z-coded moment to amplify Open What You Want.

messages.title: Head of Copy, Creative Studio
messages.location: New York City, NY; San Francisco, CA
messages.message:
The line in this role I keep rereading: connective tissue between human creative vision and AI execution infrastructure. That's the exact seam I've been living in. I built P.H.R.E.D., an operating methodology that keeps AI-assisted work connected instead of scattered, with an orchestration agent holding the source of truth and humans still owning scope and the calls you can't undo: https://lab26.randallfransen.com/phred

Before that I ran production for Base at Coinbase and led teams at AKQA on Nike and Trek, so translating dense, technical stuff into work that actually moves people isn't new ground for me. Voice, tone, quality bars that scale without going hollow, that's the part I'd want to build with your writers. Happy to talk.

insertedExample.oneHitter: Project Hub for Retrieval, Execution, & Delivery

messages.title: Chief of Staff, to the CPO
messages.message:
The AI-first part of this role is where I'd earn my keep. Most AI-in-the-org efforts scatter output everywhere: decisions in one doc, tasks with no context, conversations that dead-end. I build the operating structure first so the tools actually connect. My last project was a small, budget-tight team shipping a complex app on an aggressive timeline, and the methodology I built to make it workable is here: https://lab26.randallfransen.com/phred

Before that I ran operating cadence and program work across teams as Program Director at AKQA and Production Lead for Base at Coinbase. Quiet in the background, keeping the team on task and happy, is my natural seat. Happy to talk through how I'd approach the People org.

insertedExample.oneHitter: Project Hub for Retrieval, Execution, & Delivery
